title: Gulab Jamun
description: Soft, syrup-soaked gulab jamun without khoya or milk powder—plant-milk solids and a classic rose-cardamom sugar syrup for the festival craving.
explanation: Classic gulab jamun depends on khoya or milk powder for a porous, spongy crumb that drinks syrup. This version uses soy milk powder (or a cashew–semolina mix) so the dough fries pale-golden and soaks like the mithai-shop original.
Two non-negotiables: a soft dough that is not dry, and warm syrup so the jamuns absorb without collapsing. Do not rush the rest or the soak.
ingredients: 2–3 tbsp Neutral Oil or melted Butter (for dough)
3–5 tbsp Warm Plant Milk or Water (as needed to bind)
instructions: Make syrup first: boil sugar, water, and cardamom 6–8 minutes until slightly sticky (one-string not required—just a light syrup). Switch off, add rose water and saffron. Keep warm, not boiling.
Mix soy milk powder, maida, sooji, baking powder, and salt. Rub in oil until sandy. Add warm plant milk a spoon at a time until a soft, crack-free dough forms—do not knead hard.
Rest dough 10–12 minutes covered. Grease palms and roll smooth balls with no cracks (cracks cause oil entry and burst jamuns).
Heat oil on medium-low. Test: a tiny dough scrap should rise slowly, not brown instantly. Fry jamuns, turning gently, until deep golden-brown all over (8–12 minutes).
Drain briefly on a rack, then soak in warm syrup at least 1–2 hours (overnight is best). Serve warm or at room temperature with a little extra syrup.
stepNotes: If dough cracks while rolling, wet fingers and smooth—dry dough = hard jamuns.
High heat browns the outside and leaves the center raw; patience on medium-low is the real skill.
Syrup should be warm when jamuns go in. Ice-cold syrup will not soak; boiling syrup can break them.
Soy milk powder brands vary; if dough is sticky, dust a little maida. If dry, add milk by the teaspoon.
Leftover syrup keeps; rewarm jamuns gently in syrup before serving.

variationSections.title: No soy milk powder
items: Cashew base: fine cashew powder + 2 tbsp maida + 2 tbsp sooji + oil + plant milk—slightly denser, still excellent.
Store-bought plant mawa if available: follow pack ratios and reduce extra flour.
Baked version: brush oil and bake at 180°C until golden, then soak longer—texture differs from fried.

variationSections.title: Flavor and finish
items: Stuff a pistachio in the center before frying.
Finish with slivered almonds and a drop more rose water.
Serve with a scoop of banana nice cream for a modern plate.
